Page 1/Infrastructure Feasibility Report 32
Method of Analysis
MaaS™ securely scanned your infrastructure using an Agentless, Workload and Extended Scan methods, identifying workload components,
services, hardware, storage, and networking details of your servers/VMs. It also collected utilization and performance data for all your servers.
MaaS™ then analyzed this information and provided a range of recommendations and advisories, including Migration & Modernization
strategies, PaaS services, workload optimization, and cost modeling, to support your cloud migration journey and beyond. These insights offer
multiple options to choose the best path for moving to the cloud.
 
 
Page 1/3211-25-2025 16:34:18Report Generated Date:

Page 2/Infrastructure Feasibility Report 32
Cloud Assessment Feasibility Overview
MaaS™ has assessed the viability of your current infrastructure by analyzing compute, storage, network, and software resources based on
existing specifications and usage trends. The assessment shows that Cloud Azure East US are feasible options. Recommendations are provided
to optimize these resources for enhanced performance and cost-effectiveness.
Based on the scan of your current infrastructure, which includes 12 operating systems and server utilization showing 10 underutilized and 2
moderately utilized, MaaS recommends migrating to Azure East US Cloud for its optimal value and cost-effectiveness in managing your servers.

Cloud Resources Recommendation
MaaS™ recommends suitable cloud resources for servers, offering both Equivalence Match and Best Match options for optimal alignment based on
the captured usage trends.
Equivalence Match signifies the exact or closest available flavor in the Cloud, whereas Best Match denotes the most accurate VM sizing based on
server utilization.
Review the equivalence and best match flavors suggested by MaaS™ for the scanned source servers with its resource specifications and cost.
MaaS™ recommends flavors which is equal to or higher/lower than your existing server configuration. OS licensing cost is also included in the
Flavor Cost. Refer OS licensing Cost breakdown in TCO Analysis section for more details.
